perturbation.original_text: This ag reement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Georgia.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall terminate upon the mutual agreement of the parties, or at such time that the parties and their assigns no longer have any legal or equitable claim to anyConfidential Information. This ag reement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Georgia.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the choice of law clause to the end of the agreement could cause issues regarding enforceability, as it could be argued it was not agreed upon until the rest of the agreement was established.
perturbation.law_explanation: Georgia law requires that the parties to a contract have a meeting of the minds and mutual assent to all terms of the contract. Moving the choice of law clause to the end may imply it wasn't part of the original agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 13-1-4 - Contract defined -- Specialty contract :: 2010 Georgia Code :: US Codes and Statutes :: US Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of the Georgia Code 2024 Georgia Code 2023 Georgia Code 2022 Georgia Code 2010 Georgia Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2010 Georgia Code TITLE 13 - CONTRACTS CHAPTER 1 - GENERAL PROVISIONS § 13-1-4 - Contract defined -- Specialty contract O.C.G.A. 13-1-4 (2010) 13-1-4. Contract defined -- Specialty contract A specialty is a contract under seal and is considered by the law as entered into with more solemnity, and consequently of higher dignity, than ordinary, simple contracts. perturbation.justification: The change introduces a structural flaw by moving the choice of law clause, potentially affecting its enforceability and creating a legal contradiction. The Law Explanation says that Georgia law necessitates a 'meeting of the minds' and 'mutual assent' regarding all contract terms. By positioning the choice of law clause at the end, there is a risk that it will be perceived as not being part of the original agreement, particularly if it's argued that it was only agreed upon after other parts of the agreement were finalized. Scraped Law Snippet 1, defining a contract, highlights the importance of the agreement's solemnity and dignity, suggesting that a clear and upfront agreement on governing law is important. The structural flaw undermines the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall terminate upon the mutual agreement of the parties, or at such time that the parties and their assigns no longer have any legal or equitable claim to anyConfidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information means any information not generally known to the public, whether recorded or conveyed in written, graphic, oral or physical form, including but not limited to scientific knowledge, know-how, processes, inventions, techniques, formulae,products, business operations, customer requirements, data, original copyrighted orcopyrightable works, plans or other records, biological materials, and/or software. This agreement shall terminate upon the mutual agreement of the parties, or at such time that the parties and their assigns no longer have any legal or equitable claim to anyConfidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the termination clause into the definition of Confidential Information could be confusing. This can cause ambiguity around when and how the agreement ends. Termination should be its own section for clarity.
perturbation.law_explanation: Georgia law requires contracts to be interpreted to reflect the intentions of the parties. By placing the termination clause in a location that does not normally deal with termination clauses, it is open to different interpretations.

perturbation.justification: The original contract clearly defines a termination clause. The changed text buries the termination clause within the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This creates a structural flaw. Georgia Code § 13-2-1 states that 'The construction of a contract is a question of law for the court.' Placing the termination clause in an unexpected section introduces ambiguity and contradicts the standard structural expectations for contracts, potentially leading to misinterpretations regarding the termination conditions. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition because a critical legal term is placed in a misleading section. Because termination is a required piece of the contract, its placement in the definition of Confidential Information affects the clarity of the contract as a whole.
